content: We can frame human second-language vocabulary learning (where reflection is a core learning practice) as a lightweight, human equivalent of the LLM Self-Refine iterative self-feedback pipeline, turning passive memorization into active iterative refinement of word sense. Learners do not just memorize a dictionary definition of 'reflection' once; they repeatedly reflect on how the word fits different contexts, adjust their understanding of its nuanced usages, and converge on a more flexible, accurate mental representation over iterations.
